_metadata.description: Complete dependency graph of the residential construction workflow. Represents stages, dependencies, and execution order as a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG). Consumed by: dataset generators (Sprint 2), scheduling module (future), AI validation (Sprint 4), project progress tracking (future), analytics dashboard (future).
_metadata.rendering_hint: Use a topological sort on edges to determine valid execution order. Critical path is the longest path through the graph.

nodes.label: Site Preparation
nodes.description: Land clearing, grading, temporary utilities, permits obtained.
nodes.completion_marker: Site cleared, graded, and temporary utilities in place

nodes.label: Foundation
nodes.description: Excavation, footings, foundation walls or slab-on-grade.
nodes.completion_marker: Foundation complete, backfill done, concrete cured 7 days

nodes.label: Concrete Flatwork
nodes.description: Garage slab, interior slabs, driveways, walkways.
nodes.completion_marker: All concrete flatwork poured and cured
nodes.notes: Can run in parallel with early framing on a different section of site.

nodes.label: Framing
nodes.description: All structural framing including floors, walls, and roof structure.
nodes.completion_marker: All structural framing complete, sheathing applied

nodes.label: Building Dried-In
nodes.description: Roof underlayment and sheathing applied. Building is weathertight enough for interior work to begin.
nodes.completion_marker: Roof underlayment installed, windows and doors rough openings weather protected

nodes.label: Roofing
nodes.description: Complete roofing system installation.
nodes.completion_marker: Shingles, flashing, ridge cap, and ridge vent complete
nodes.notes: Not on critical path for interior work sequence once dried-in milestone is achieved, but must complete before final.

nodes.label: Electrical Rough-In
nodes.description: All wiring, panel, and electrical boxes installed inside walls.
nodes.completion_marker: All circuits rough-in complete, inspection passed

nodes.label: HVAC Rough-In
nodes.description: All ductwork, refrigerant lines, and ventilation installed inside walls.
nodes.completion_marker: All duct runs complete, refrigerant lines run, inspection passed
nodes.notes: Same sequence_order as electrical_rough_in — they run in parallel.

nodes.label: Plumbing Rough-In
nodes.description: All drain, vent, and supply piping installed inside walls.
nodes.completion_marker: All pipes run, pressure test passed, inspection passed
nodes.notes: Same sequence_order as electrical_rough_in — they run in parallel.

nodes.label: All Rough-Ins Complete
nodes.description: All three rough-in trades have finished and all three inspections have passed. Ready to proceed to insulation.
nodes.completion_marker: Electrical, HVAC, and plumbing rough-in inspections all passed

nodes.label: Insulation
nodes.description: Batt, blown, or spray foam insulation in all exterior walls, attic, and relevant interior walls.
nodes.completion_marker: All insulation installed, inspection passed

nodes.label: Drywall
nodes.description: Gypsum board hung, taped, finished (3 coats), sanded, and textured.
nodes.completion_marker: All drywall hung, finished, sanded, and ready for prime

nodes.label: Painting
nodes.description: Primer and finish paint coats on all interior surfaces.
nodes.completion_marker: All rooms primed and painted, trim coated

nodes.label: Tile Work
nodes.description: Ceramic or porcelain tile in bathrooms, kitchen, and other areas.
nodes.completion_marker: All tile set, grouted, and sealed
nodes.notes: Can begin during late painting or after painting. Often runs parallel.

nodes.label: Flooring
nodes.description: Hardwood, LVP, laminate, or carpet installation in non-tiled areas.
nodes.completion_marker: All flooring installed in all rooms

nodes.label: Cabinets and Countertops
nodes.description: Kitchen and bathroom cabinet installation, followed by countertop template, fabrication, and installation.
nodes.completion_marker: All cabinets installed and leveled, countertops installed
nodes.notes: 21 days includes the 14-day countertop fabrication lead time after templating. This is often on the critical path.

nodes.label: Trim and Millwork
nodes.description: Baseboards, crown molding, door casings, window stools, and interior doors.
nodes.completion_marker: All trim installed, caulked, and painted

nodes.label: Electrical Finish
nodes.description: Install outlets, switches, light fixtures, ceiling fans, smoke detectors.
nodes.completion_marker: All devices, fixtures, and covers installed. Panel energized.

nodes.label: HVAC Finish
nodes.description: Install equipment (air handler, condenser), register grilles, commission system.
nodes.completion_marker: Equipment installed, system commissioned, airflow balanced

nodes.label: Plumbing Finish
nodes.description: Install toilets, sinks, faucets, water heater, shower/tub fixtures.
nodes.completion_marker: All fixtures installed, water tested, no leaks

nodes.label: Punch List
nodes.description: Walk-through to identify deficiencies, then complete all corrections before final inspection.
nodes.completion_marker: All punch list items corrected and verified

nodes.label: Final Inspection
nodes.description: Building department final inspection. If passed, Certificate of Occupancy is issued.
nodes.completion_marker: Final inspection passed, CO issued

nodes.label: Project Closeout
nodes.description: Final client walkthrough, documentation, warranty information, lien waivers, final payment.
nodes.completion_marker: Client accepts, final payment received, all documents delivered

edges.description: Site must be prepared and permits obtained before excavation.

edges.lag_reason: Concrete must cure for at least 7 days before framing loads are applied.
edges.description: Foundation must be structurally complete before framing can begin.
Concrete flatwork (garage slab, etc.) can begin after foundation walls are up. Under-slab plumbing must be in first.
Dried-in milestone is reached when roof framing and underlayment are complete, even if wall sheathing is not fully done.
Roofing can begin as soon as roof framing (trusses/rafters and decking) is complete.
Electrical rough-in can begin once the building is dried-in. Does not require full framing completion.
HVAC rough-in can begin once dried-in milestone is achieved.
Plumbing rough-in can begin once dried-in milestone is achieved.
Electrical rough-in must complete AND pass inspection.
HVAC rough-in must complete AND pass inspection.
Plumbing rough-in must complete AND pass inspection.
All rough-in inspections must pass before insulation can be installed.
Insulation must be complete AND pass inspection before drywall.
Drywall must be fully finished (hung, taped, sanded) before painting begins.
Tile work in wet areas can start after drywall/cement board backer is installed in those areas.
Painting must be substantially complete before flooring is installed.
Cabinet installation can begin after prime coat on walls. Finish coat happens around installed cabinets.
Trim carpentry can begin after first paint coat in a room.
Base molding is installed after flooring so it sits on top and covers the expansion gap.
Electrical finish devices are installed into the boxes set during rough-in, which are now accessible through drywall cutouts.
HVAC finish (grilles, equipment) requires drywall to be complete so equipment platforms and grille openings are defined.
Plumbing finish (toilets, sinks, faucets) requires drywall complete and painting to be substantially done.
Kitchen sink and dishwasher connections cannot be made until kitchen countertops are installed.
Electrical finish must be complete before punch list walk.
HVAC finish must be complete before punch list.
Plumbing finish must be complete before punch list.
Flooring must be complete before punch list.
Trim work must be complete before punch list.
All cabinets and countertops must be installed before punch list.
All tile work must be complete before punch list.
Roofing must be complete before final.
All punch list items must be corrected before calling for final inspection.
Final inspection must pass (Certificate of Occupancy issued) before project closeout.

critical_path.description: The longest path through the project. Delay on any critical path node delays project completion.
critical_path.note: HVAC rough-in (7 days) is slightly longer than electrical (6 days) on the parallel path. In practice, HVAC often sets the rough-in completion date.

parallel_groups.name: Three Rough-In Trades
parallel_groups.description: Electrical, HVAC, and plumbing rough-in all run simultaneously after dried-in milestone.
parallel_groups.bottleneck_note: The longest-running trade determines when the parallel group completes. HVAC typically takes longest.

parallel_groups.name: Finishing Sub-Trades
parallel_groups.description: Multiple finish trades run simultaneously after painting is substantially complete.
parallel_groups.bottleneck_note: Cabinets + countertop fabrication lead time typically the bottleneck.

parallel_groups.name: Roofing and Rough-In Overlap
parallel_groups.description: Roofing can continue on exterior while rough-in trades work inside.
parallel_groups.overlap_condition: Can overlap — exterior and interior work do not conflict.

blocked_stages.blocking_reason: Structural prerequisite — cannot build structure without base
No structural deck to attach roofing to
Insulation would permanently cover uninspected rough-in work
Insulation inspection is a code requirement before cover-up
No surfaces to paint until drywall exists
Paint drips and foot traffic damage finished floors
Cannot legally occupy building without Certificate of Occupancy

optional_stages.optional_when: Site is already cleared and permitted at purchase
Project has no additional flatwork beyond the foundation slab
No tile specified in the project design
optional_stages.impact_if_skipped: Different flooring type used in wet areas

topological_sort.description: One valid execution order of all stages. Any valid topological sort of the DAG is an acceptable execution plan.
